components.name: oelt-mcq
components.a11y: Light DOM, fully keyboard-operable, visible focus, screen-reader documented in its spec, axe-clean, honors prefers-reduced-motion.
components.example:
Which standard does cmi5 build on?

SCORM 1.2 xAPI AICC

Right — cmi5 is an xAPI profile.

Not quite — cmi5 is built on xAPI.

Select every accessibility requirement (choose all that apply).

Keyboard operable Mouse only Visible focus

How confident are you?

Low High

components.name: oelt-text-entry
components.a11y: the prompt is the input's `<label>`; on submit, focus moves to the `aria-live="polite"` feedback and correctness is conveyed in text (visually-hidden "Correct"/"Incorrect" prefix), never colour alone. Numeric mode uses `inputmode="decimal"` on a text input (not `type="number"`) for a numeric soft keyboard without spinbutton/locale pitfalls.
components.example:
What is the capital of France?

Correct — Paris.

Not quite — it's Paris.

Estimate π to two decimal places.

In a word, how did this lesson feel?

components.name: oelt-quiz
components.a11y: a `::part(status)` live region announces progress ("Answered 1 of 2 questions") and the final outcome ("Quiz complete. Score 75%."); pooled-out questions get `hidden`.
components.example:
Which standard is recommended for new content?

SCORM 1.2 cmi5

Name that standard (one word).
